title: Italian Wedding Soup
cScaleSummary: C2 because lean ground chicken is the primary cholesterol-contributing ingredient, with a small amount of Parmesan contributing only a minor additional amount. The egg white, breadcrumbs, vegetables, pasta, escarole, olive oil, lemon juice, herbs, and seasonings contribute little to no cholesterol, keeping the estimated cholesterol per serving within the C2 range.
ldlActiveSummary: Not LDL-Active because the recipe is centered on animal protein. It remains a lower-cholesterol C2 soup because it uses lean chicken, egg white, broth, vegetables, greens, and a restrained amount of Parmesan while avoiding butter, cream, high-fat meat, and heavy cheese, but it does not qualify as actively LDL-lowering under the EWHF standard.
intro: Italian wedding soup comes from the older idea of minestra maritata, or married soup, where greens and meat are brought together in one pot. The American-Italian version became lighter, smaller, and more broth-forward, with tiny meatballs, tender greens, and small pasta. A good version should feel clear, savory, and restorative rather than heavy. The meatballs bring the richness, the greens keep it grounded, and the broth ties the whole thing together.

stepGroups.title: Make the Meatballs
items: Combine the ground chicken breast ({{quantity:ground-chicken-breast}}), egg white ({{quantity:egg-white}}), plain breadcrumbs ({{quantity:plain-breadcrumbs}}), Parmesan cheese ({{quantity:parmesan-meatballs}}), parsley ({{quantity:parsley-meatballs}}), garlic ({{quantity:garlic-meatballs}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-meatballs}}), black pepper ({{quantity:black-pepper-meatballs}}), and ground nutmeg ({{quantity:ground-nutmeg}}) in a bowl.
Mix gently until just combined. Do not overwork the mixture or the meatballs will turn dense.
Roll into small meatballs, about 3/4 inch across. The recipe should make roughly 36 to 42 small meatballs.
Place the meatballs on a parchment-lined tray and refrigerate for 15 minutes while the soup base is started.

stepGroups.title: Build the Soup Base
items: Heat the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil}}) in a large soup pot over medium heat.

Add the onion ({{quantity:yellow-onion}}), carrot ({{quantity:carrot}}), celery ({{quantity:celery}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-soup-base}}), and black pepper ({{quantity:black-pepper-soup-base}}).
Cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring often, until the vegetables soften and the onion turns translucent but not browned.
Add the garlic ({{quantity:garlic-soup-base}}) and cook for 1 minute.

stepGroups.title: Start the Broth
items: Add the chicken stock ({{quantity:chicken-stock}}) and Parmesan rind ({{quantity:parmesan-rind}}).
Bring to a gentle simmer.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Meatballs
items: Lower the heat so the soup is barely simmering.
Add the chilled meatballs one at a time.
Simmer gently for 8 to 10 minutes, until the meatballs are cooked through and firm.
Do not boil hard. A rough boil can make the meatballs tough and cloud the broth.

stepGroups.title: Finish the Soup
items: Add the acini di pepe ({{quantity:acini-di-pepe}}) and simmer until just tender, about 8 to 9 minutes.
Add the escarole ({{quantity:escarole}}) and simmer for 3 to 4 minutes, until wilted and tender but still green.
Remove the Parmesan rind.
Stir in the lemon juice ({{quantity:lemon-juice}}) and parsley ({{quantity:parsley-finish}}).
Taste and adjust only with salt or lemon juice as needed.

stepGroups.title: Serve
items: Serve hot, with small meatballs, escarole, pasta, and clear broth in each bowl.
